description: 17 - 16-01-2025 - Emails between Gerard McGurk (Head of Security & Resilience Department, FCDO) and the UK Security Vetting Team, CO
text:
Department, FCDO) and the UK Security Vetting Team, CO

--- Forwarded message ---------

Fro: JCS JCS >
Date: Thu, 16 Jan 2025 at 15:35

Subject: Fwd: Urgent vetting question

To: Vincent Devine < Personal >

Latest email on Lord M

Forwarded message
iichael Roberts < Personal s
Date: Thu, 16 Jan 2025 at 14:31

Subject: Fwd: Urgent vetting question

To: JCS JCS =
Fyi

mecencc ene Forwarded message ---------

Frm: JCS = < JCS >

Date: Thu, 16 Jan 2025, 14:27
Subject: Re: Urgent vetting question

To: Gerard McGurk < Personal >

Gc: JCS Personal , Ian Collard * < Personal =
JCS Personal , Tom Bramley

< Personal >, Patricia Dreghorn

< Personal >, Michael Roberts

< Personal >

Hello

Thave just had a call with the VCO and our case team. The case is progressing and both a referee
and line manager have now been interviewed. Whilst the interview with Lord M was scheduled
for tomorrow we still await a number of outstanding documents and information. The VCO is in
contact with JCS and they have agreed that it is
possible to reschedule the interview for next Friday, at which point all missing information
should be provided. We are told that this fits with the other diary commitments and is
acceptable and so we are awaiting confirmation on this from JCS later this aftemoon.

We are not seeking to delay unnecessarily but there is a need to ensure that the interview adds
value and currently I understand we do not have sufficient information in some areas to be able
to do that.

46

I have asked for a further update as soon as we hear back from 
thanks
On Thu, 16 Jan 2025 at 13:49, Gerard McGurk <
> wrote:
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - PERSONAL DATA
Thanks 
. The primary issue appears to be one of time – or the lack of it 
relative to Lord Mandelson trying to get 101 things done in a very short period of time 
(including an audience with the King) before he departs for Washington. I think he 
essentially has a two week window to get everything done before flying to the US. 
We have made the point to him about being upfront and detailed about his contacts.
Gerard
OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE - PERSONAL DATA
From: 
 
Sent: 16 January 2025 13:32
To: 
 
Cc: Gerard McGurk <
>; Ian Collard * <
>; 
 
; Tom Bramley 
<
>; Patricia Dreghorn <
>
Subject: Re: Urgent vetting question
Good afternoon
I am urgently seeking an update on this from the VCO involved and I will come back as soon 
as possible to clarify the situation.
thanks
Personal

On Thu, 16 Jan 2025 at 13:26, JCS < JCS > wrote:

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Gerard

This is the reply when | chased yesterday on whether an interview had been
arranged:

Please see below response from the Special Handling Team.

We are still waiting for documents to be completed that are required prior to the interview.
As far as I know, the VCO is looking to get him booked in next week hopefitlly for the
interview, but this is dependent on us receiving all that we need.

JCS

JCS | Head of Personnel Security Team
Cluster 4 Security, Estates Security and Resilence Department, ESND

Personal | Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office, King Charles
St | London | SW1A 2AH

Telephone: Mobile: Personal | email: Personal

A-Z Guidance - Developed Vetting (DV) and Security Check (SC) clearances

48


OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

From: Gerard McGurk < Personal >

Sent: 16 January 2025 13:07

To: lan Collard * < Personal >; JCS Personal ;
JCS Personal

Cc: Tom Bramley < Personal >; Patricia Dreghorn

< Personal Es

Subject: RE: Urgent vetting question

OFFICIAL-SENSITIVE

Dear JCS and colleagues,

Our FCDO Americas Director has told us there is a possibility that Lord Mandelson’s
DV interview tomorrow may be cancelled because UKSV require more information
on who he knows. Lord Mandelson will be looking to try to expand on this
requirement later this afternoon.

It remains important the interview happens tomorrow. There’s not another slot and
UKSV colleagues talking through with him during the interview will help get answers,
with the possibility of follow up at a later stage.

Could you confirm the DV interview will still proceed?

Thanks.

Gerard

49


Gerard McGurk (he/him) MBE|Deputy Director and Senior Security Adviser

Head of Security & Resilience Department | Estates, Security & Network
Directorate | Personal | Personal for diary requests |

Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Office King Charles St | London | SW1A
2AH

Email: Personal | Available on Teams or Mobile Personal

parsing_notes: Extract structured email headers (From, To, Cc, Subject, Date/Sent) for each message in the chain. Identify reply/forward nesting depth. Parse each message body separately. Redacted addresses appear as 'PERSONAL' — names are retained. Build sender→recipient edges per message for communication graph.

messages.subject: Urgent vetting question
messages.body:
Dear JCS and colleagues,

Our FCDO Americas Director has told us there is a possibility that Lord Mandelson’s
DV interview tomorrow may be cancelled because UKSV require more information
on who he knows. Lord Mandelson will be looking to try to expand on this
requirement later this afternoon.

It remains important the interview happens tomorrow. There’s not another slot and
UKSV colleagues talking through with him during the interview will help get answers,
with the possibility of follow up at a later stage.

Could you confirm the DV interview will still proceed?

Thanks.

Gerard

49
